summary: Created complete task registry system with SQLite database schema, initialized database with sample data, and Python module with full CRUD operations. The system supports domain/project hierarchy, priority buckets (strategic, external, urgent, normal), source linking (conversation, meeting, manual, email), task events log for latency tracking, and helper views. All required functions implemented and tested.

design_decisions: Used SQLite for simplicity and local operation - no external dependencies
Implemented views (v_pending_tasks, v_task_latency, v_tasks_today) for common queries
Event log (task_events table) provides full audit trail for latency analysis
Parent_task_id enables subtask/milestone hierarchies without complexity
Plan_json field stores adjustable plans of action as JSON for flexibility
validation: Created 3 test tasks across 3 domains (Personal, Zo, Careerspan)
Verified get_tasks_for_today() returns tasks with correct priority ordering
Verified get_task_by_source() successfully retrieves task by conversation_id
Verified complete_task() logs completion event and tracks actual time
Verified calculate_latency_stats() works with completed tasks
considerations_for_other_drops: The Python module (task_registry.py) is the canonical interface - other drops should import and use these functions
Priority bucket ordering: urgent > strategic > external > normal (implemented in views)
Domain and project auto-creation happens on task creation if they don't exist
Source linking is designed to support conversation_id lookups from Zo system
Latency stats can be filtered by domain/project for capacity estimation
schema_extensions: Consider adding tags table if V wants tag-based filtering
Consider adding task_dependencies table for dependency tracking beyond parent/child
Views can be extended as new query patterns emerge
